content: To prevent oxalate absorption, Chris recommends consuming 3 to 400 mg of calcium per meal.
Calcium binds to oxalate in the intestines, preventing its absorption into the bloodstream.
Chris suggests maintaining urine pH between 6.4 and 6.8 to prevent calcium oxalate crystallization.
Good alkalinizing foods include potassium salts, such as potassium citrate or malate, which can help maintain urine pH.
Sodium bicarbonate can increase urine pH, but it is considered a temporary measure due to potential digestion issues.
Chris recommends getting potassium from food sources as the best long-term solution for alkalinizing urine.
